suggestedPatch.target: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the discipline of making your content eligible for direct citation, mention, or use in answers returned by AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the discipline of making your content eligible for direct citation, mention, or use in answers returned by AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: AEO is just getting your content quoted by ChatGPT and friends instead of ranked on Google—same game, different referee.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Sounds like a specific human" scored 3/10: Mostly committee-voice with a few jarring first-person interjections that don't match the surrounding corporate tone.

evidence: Genuine opinions 5/10 — A few spicy takes exist ('most AEO advice is recycled SEO') but they're drowned in safe, hedged claims.

suggestedPatch.target: Demonstrating expertise, experience, authoritativeness, and trustworthiness (E-E-A-T) remains essential.
suggestedPatch.before: Demonstrating expertise, experience, authoritativeness, and trustworthiness (E-E-A-T) remains essential.
suggestedPatch.after: E-E-A-T still matters, but honestly it's become a checkbox—LLMs care more about whether you have a number nobody else has.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions" scored 5/10: A few spicy takes exist ('most AEO advice is recycled SEO') but they're drowned in safe, hedged claims.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Magpie metaphor and 'cheapest-to-cite source' are fresh, but most sentences read like template filler.

suggestedPatch.target: A successful AEO strategy in 2026 includes monitoring brand citations in LLM answers, injecting schema.org ‘DefinedTerm’ and fact structures, publishing original statistics, and earning authoritative coverage.
suggestedPatch.before: A successful AEO strategy in 2026 includes monitoring brand citations in LLM answers, injecting schema.org ‘DefinedTerm’ and fact structures, publishing original statistics, and earning authoritative coverage.
suggestedPatch.after: Winning AEO in 2026 means three boring habits: watch your citations weekly, mark up definitions, and publish stats worth stealing.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: Magpie metaphor and 'cheapest-to-cite source' are fresh, but most sentences read like template filler.

evidence: Specific citations 6/10 — Has one concrete stat (34% more Perplexity citations, 90 days) and dated sources, but no named companies, people, prices, or specific examples.
Point of view 4/10 — Largely surveys the topic neutrally; the few opinionated lines feel bolted onto a balanced explainer.

suggestedPatch.target: This table clarifies tactical differences and areas of overlap between AEO and SEO for B2B SaaS brands targeting generative engine visibility.
suggestedPatch.before: This table clarifies tactical differences and areas of overlap between AEO and SEO for B2B SaaS brands targeting generative engine visibility.

evidence: Quotable sentence 5/10 — The magpie line and 'cheapest-to-cite source' are quotable, but they're surrounded by forgettable definitional prose.
Intro earns attention 2/10 — Opens with boilerplate TL;DR, byline metadata, and a textbook definition—zero hook, reader has no reason to continue.

suggestedPatch.target: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the strategy for ranking brands and content in AI-generated answers from engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the strategy for ranking brands and content in AI-generated answers from engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Our ChatGPT citations doubled last year while Google traffic flatlined. Here's the uncomfortable playbook that actually caused it.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 2/10: Opens with boilerplate TL;DR, byline metadata, and a textbook definition—zero hook, reader has no reason to continue.

evidence: intro_hook 5/10 — The post's actual opening sentences (after the metadata block) are a dry definitional statement and a table-of-contents promise. Neither creates urgency or surprise. The TL;DR above it is functional but buried in a UI label. The hook needs a concrete stake—a number, a disruption, a tension—to earn the scroll.

suggestedPatch.target: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the strategy for ranking brands and content in AI-generated answers from engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the strategy for ranking brands and content in AI-generated answers from engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Google sent 2.1 billion fewer clicks to websites in Q1 2026 than Q1 2024—not because people stopped searching, but because ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini answered before anyone clicked. That's the problem Answer Engine Optimization exists to solve.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 5/10: The post's actual opening sentences (after the metadata block) are a dry definitional statement and a table-of-contents promise. Neither creates urgency or surprise. The TL;DR above it is functional but buried in a UI label. The hook needs a concrete stake—a number, a disruption, a tension—to earn the scroll.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — The post has one strong specific data point (the Semrush 34% Perplexity citation lift over 90 days) but much of the tactical advice floats in abstract verbs: 'audit,' 'track,' 'optimize,' 'monitor.' The AEO playbook section lists steps without naming a single tool, threshold, cadence, or example brand. The key-takeaways and summary repeat the same abstractions. Concrete nouns—tool names, query examples, specific schema snippet, a named brand outcome—are almost entirely absent outside the one case-study stat.

suggestedPatch.target: Brands must audit, track, and optimize their visibility and sentiment in content surfaced by AI answer engines.
suggestedPatch.before: Brands must audit, track, and optimize their visibility and sentiment in content surfaced by AI answer engines.
suggestedPatch.after: Brands should run weekly prompts in ChatGPT-4o, Perplexity Pro, and Gemini Advanced—logging whether their brand is cited, quoted by name, or absent entirely—then compare mention-share against two named competitors using a tracking spreadsheet or a tool like Profound or Otterly.ai.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The post has one strong specific data point (the Semrush 34% Perplexity citation lift over 90 days) but much of the tactical advice floats in abstract verbs: 'audit,' 'track,' 'optimize,' 'monitor.' The AEO playbook section lists steps without naming a single tool, threshold, cadence, or example brand. The key-takeaways and summary repeat the same abstractions. Concrete nouns—tool names, query examples, specific schema snippet, a named brand outcome—are almost entirely absent outside the one case-study stat.
